test_description: For each masked nside=256 map, fit and subtract the monopole and dipole on unmasked pixels, set masked pixels to zero only for a spherical-harmonic transform, reconstruct a low-l map retaining 2<=ell<=30, then reapply the mask. In ecliptic coordinates split unmasked pixels into an ecliptic belt |beta|<30 deg and complementary caps |beta|>=30 deg. Compute the pixel-area-weighted variance in each region after subtracting that region's own mean, V_belt and V_cap, and use the single scalar S=ln(V_belt/V_cap). Compare the SMICA value of S with the 1000 simulation values using the identical pipeline.
test_hypothesis: The Planck low-l ecliptic belt-to-cap variance log-ratio is anomalous relative to simulations, meaning the ecliptic belt variance differs unusually from the cap variance.
justificaton: The statistic treats both directions as potentially anomalous: a large positive S indicates enhanced belt variance, while a large negative S indicates suppressed belt variance. The code reports both upper- and lower-tail probabilities plus a two-sided p-value, and there is no clear directional preference specified.

custom_summary.test_description: Low-l Ecliptic Belt-to-Cap Variance Log-Ratio: fit and subtract the monopole and dipole on unmasked pixels; zero-fill masked pixels only for a spherical-harmonic transform; reconstruct the 2<=ell<=30 map; reapply the mask; split unmasked pixels into ecliptic belt |beta|<30 deg and caps; return S=ln(V_belt/V_cap) after subtracting each region's own mean.
custom_summary.statistic: S = ln(V_belt/V_cap) for the 2<=ell<=30 masked, monopole/dipole-subtracted reconstruction; V_belt and V_cap are equal-pixel-area variances after subtracting each region's own mean.
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail means enhanced low-l variance in the ecliptic belt relative to the ecliptic caps; lower tail means suppressed belt variance relative to the caps
custom_summary.masked_pixel_handling: np.nan pixels are ignored for fitting and regional variances, and are replaced by 0.0 only for the healpy spherical-harmonic transform

plot_spec.title: Low-l ecliptic belt-to-cap variance log-ratio
plot_spec.caption: Distribution of the 2<=ell<=30 ecliptic belt-to-cap variance log-ratio from simulations, using the same mask, monopole/dipole subtraction, zero-filled transform, low-l reconstruction, and regional-mean subtraction as for Planck.

custom_summary.planck_direction: negative: the low-l ecliptic-belt variance is below the cap variance in this map
custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in either 5% tail of the empirical simulation distribution for this low-l ecliptic belt-to-cap variance log-ratio.

test_summary.Description: SMICA and 1000 simulations were monopole/dipole-subtracted on the unmasked sky, filtered to 2<=ell<=30, re-masked, and split in ecliptic coordinates into a belt |beta|<30 deg and caps |beta|>=30 deg. The statistic was S=ln(V_belt/V_cap), using regional mean-subtracted variances.
test_summary.Results: Planck gives S=-0.129, i.e. V_belt/V_cap=0.879. Simulations have mean S=0.004, std=0.209, and Planck lies at about z=-0.64 / 0.63 sigma, with two-tailed p=0.528 and percentile about 26%.
test_summary.Interpretation: The low-l ecliptic belt variance is mildly lower than the cap variance, but this is common in LCDM simulations and is far from a 2 sigma or p<0.05 anomaly.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: Related real-space variance anomalies are well documented, especially ecliptic hemispherical variance/power asymmetry: O’Dwyer, Copi, Knox & Starkman, “CMB-S4 and the Hemispherical Variance Anomaly” (2016), and O’Dwyer et al., “Hemispherical Variance Anomaly and Reionization Optical Depth” (2019), report anomalously low northern ecliptic variance in temperature. This test instead compares an equatorial ecliptic belt to both caps and finds no significant excess or suppression.
